file_name: NETGEAR,INC_04_21_2003-EX-10.16- AMENDMENT #2 TO THE DISTRIBUTION AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: During the initial one year period beginning on the Amendment Date, Distributor shall be the only distributor appointed by NETGEAR in [*], subject to Distributor conducting mutually agreed to marketing activities as described in the Marketing Plan for [*] to be developed and agreed to by and between the parties and which shall be attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 4a.
perturbation.changed_text: During the initial one year period beginning on the Amendment Date, Distributor shall be the primary distributor appointed by NETGEAR in [*]. NETGEAR reserves the right to appoint additional distributors at its sole discretion during this period. The execution of marketing activities will not impact the exclusivity of the distributor appointment.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Distributor shall be the only distributor during the initial period, implying exclusivity. The changed text introduces 'primary distributor' while also giving NETGEAR the right to appoint additional distributors, thus directly contradicting the implied exclusivity. Also states that the execution of marketing activities will not impact the exclusivity of the distributor appointment, even though originally it depends on the marketing activities agreed upon.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the initial one year period beginning on the Amendment Date, Distributor shall be the primary distributor appointed by NETGEAR in [*]. NETGEAR reserves the right to appoint additional distributors at its sole discretion during this period. The execution of marketing activities will not impact the exclusivity of the distributor appointment. For the purposes of this provision, distributor shall mean a company acquiring products directly from NETGEAR for resale or license to dealers or other second tier resellers which in turn resell or license the products to end use customers. The foregoing notwithstanding, during the [*] and any subsequent period, NETGEAR reserves the right to sell or license Products in [*] to customers other than distributors such as, but not limited to resellers who procure Products at centralized locations for resale to end-use customers solely through their wholly or majority owned retail outlets, both store-front and catalog.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines the distributor role from 'only' to 'primary' and explicitly states NETGEAR can appoint additional distributors. The original text implies exclusivity. This directly contradicts the idea of 'only' distributor, creating an in-text contradiction, as now, based on the changed text, it's NETGEAR's prerogative to add more distributors, which wasn't the case in the original text. The statement that the execution of marketing activities will not impact the exclusivity of the distributor appointment also contradicts the original agreement of performing certain marketing activities agreed upon by both parties.

perturbation.original_text: Following the [*], for any extension or renewal term, Distributor's appointment as Distributor shall be non-exclusive and NETGEAR may appoint other distributors in [*] at its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: Following the [*], for any extension or renewal term, Distributor's appointment as Distributor may or may not be exclusive and NETGEAR may appoint other distributors in [*] at its sole discretion, subject to the Distributor's performance and NETGEAR's business needs as solely determined by NETGEAR.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states clearly that distributor appointment is non-exclusive after the specified time. The modified version introduces ambiguity by stating 'may or may not be exclusive' which directly contradicts the original intent and makes the enforcement uncertain. This change also introduces performance and business needs as factors, providing NETGEAR wide latitude.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms and conditions of this Amendment, shall amend and supersede any conflicting terms of the original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement shall remain unchanged.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly stated non-exclusivity for distributor appointments after a specific period. The changed text introduces ambiguity ('may or may not be exclusive') regarding exclusivity. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text which states that the terms of the amendment supersede conflicting terms in the original agreement. The introduction of ambiguity creates a direct contradiction, as the original agreement aimed for clear non-exclusivity after a period, now made uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: The parties have agreed to amend their Distribution Agreement ("Agreement") dated October 1,1996.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties have agreed to potentially amend their Distribution Agreement ("Agreement") dated October 1,1996, subject to further negotiations and written consent from both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the parties *have agreed* to amend their distribution agreement. The modified text changes this to *potentially agree*, adding a contingency for 'further negotiations and written consent.' This introduces ambiguity about whether the amendment is actually in effect and weakens the contract's enforceability. This contradicts all the subsequent sections that assume the amendment is valid and in place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2, TERRITORY, is amended to be United States, [*].
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly stated that the parties *have agreed* to amend the agreement, providing a definitive basis for subsequent sections relying on this amendment. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating the parties *potentially agree*, contingent on further negotiations and written consent. This directly contradicts Section 2, which is amended based on the *agreed* amendment. The changed text undermines the certainty required for the amendment to take effect, creating a strong contradiction that impacts enforceability. Because the potential amendment is now contingent, the amended Section 2 becomes potentially invalid, creating a direct conflict between the initial agreement and the subsequent amendment.
